title: Add gRPC client with deadline propagation
description: Implements a gRPC client for the recommendation service with deadline/timeout support.
diff:
diff --git a/clients/grpc_client.py b/clients/grpc_client.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d2e3f4a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/clients/grpc_client.py
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+import grpc
+from proto import recommendation_pb2, recommendation_pb2_grpc
+
+class RecommendationClient:
+    def __init__(self, host: str, port: int) -> None:
+        self._channel = grpc.insecure_channel(f"{host}:{port}")
+        self._stub = recommendation_pb2_grpc.RecommendationStub(self._channel)
+
+    def get_recommendations(self, user_id: int, timeout: float = 5.0) -> list:
+        request = recommendation_pb2.RecommendRequest(user_id=user_id)
+        # Local timeout, but doesn't propagate deadline to downstream services
+        # The recommendation service calls the ML model service with its own timeout
+        # If our timeout is 5s but the downstream chain is 5s + 5s = 10s,
+        # we'll timeout but downstream services continue processing (resource waste)
+        response = self._stub.GetRecommendations(request, timeout=timeout)
+        return list(response.items)
comments: xena: The timeout is local only — it doesn't propagate as a gRPC deadline to downstream services. If our 5s timeout fires, the recommendation service's call to the ML service continues running (wasting resources). Use grpc deadline propagation via metadata.
yuri: Also, the channel is insecure (insecure_channel). Internal gRPC should use TLS.
author-zara: I'll add deadline propagation and switch to secure_channel.
author: zara
